fragility_points: Full readable rules text was not accessible in the fetch, leaving some source-of-truth ambiguity.
The contextual price snapshot may stale quickly in a fast crypto market.
The disagreement is modest and mostly about residual execution risk, not a strong opposite thesis.
key_assumptions: The market resolves on a qualifying intraperiod touch rather than a weekly close.
The accessible TradingView snapshot is directionally representative of current spot conditions.
Round-number resistance can still block a touch over a short window even after a near miss.
main_logical_chain: The market prices the $2,400 touch as very likely.
Independent contextual evidence indicates ETH is already very close to the threshold.
That supports a yes lean, but the threshold also sits at visible resistance within a short time window.
Therefore the market is probably right directionally but somewhat too confident, yielding an ~80% estimate instead of ~89-92%.
main_thesis: ETH likely reaches $2,400 during the window, but the market is somewhat overconfident because a short-dated round-number resistance can still preserve meaningful miss risk.
source_quality_view: Primary source quality is good for market baseline but incomplete for full settlement mechanics in accessible fetch; contextual price evidence is useful but not authoritative.
strongest_disconfirmers: If the near-$2,395 context is accurate and current, one ordinary bullish intraday move could settle the market quickly.
$2,400 was explicitly framed as nearby resistance, so repeated rejection remains plausible.
strongest_supports: ETH was contextually described above roughly $2,350 with a spike near $2,395, leaving only a small move to $2,400.
The market baseline itself is extremely high, indicating broad consensus that the threshold is reachable.
timing_relevance: Very high: this is a short-dated threshold market where a single intraday move can settle the outcome.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact governing print methodology in the Polymarket rules section.
Whether broader crypto catalysts emerge during the remaining window.
what_would_change_view: A verified qualifying print above $2,400, full readable rules confirming an easier trigger, or stronger independent evidence of active breakout momentum would move the estimate upward.
